sections.label: Section 1606
sections.translation: And the one who honors his mother ?

sections.label: Section 1606b
sections.translation: He was born fortunate .

sections.label: Section 1607
sections.translation: Not like you !

sections.label: Section 1607b
sections.translation: No , for I do not delight in these wicked women .

sections.label: Section 1608
sections.translation: Remove that sword from my daughter !

sections.label: Section 1608b
sections.translation: You are a liar .

sections.label: Section 1609
sections.translation: Will you kill my daughter ?

sections.label: Section 1609b
sections.translation: Now you are not a liar !

sections.label: Section 1610
sections.translation: Ah me ! what shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 1610b
sections.translation: Go to the Argives and persuade them —

sections.label: Section 1611
sections.translation: Persuade them what ?

sections.label: Section 1611b
sections.translation: Not to kill us ; entreat the city .

sections.label: Section 1612
sections.translation: Or you will slay my child ?

sections.label: Section 1612b
sections.translation: That is correct .

sections.label: Section 1613
sections.translation: O wretched Helen —

sections.label: Section 1613b
sections.translation: Am I not wretched ?

sections.label: Section 1614
sections.translation: I brought you back from Troy to be a victim —

sections.label: Section 1614b
sections.translation: If only she had been !

sections.label: Section 1615
sections.translation: After innumerable troubles .

sections.label: Section 1615b
sections.translation: Except where I was concerned .

sections.label: Section 1616
sections.translation: I have suffered dreadfully !

sections.label: Section 1616b
sections.translation: Yes , for you would not help me then .

sections.label: Section 1617
sections.translation: You have me there .

sections.label: Section 1617b
sections.original: σαυτὸν σύ γʼ ἔλαβες κακὸς γεγώς .
sections.translation: Your own cowardice has you . Calling from the roof to Electra Fire the palace from beneath , Electra ; and , Pylades , my most trusty friend ,

sections.label: Section 1620
sections.translation: kindle the parapet of these walls . The palace is seen to be ablaze .

sections.label: Section 1621
sections.original: ὦ γαῖα Δαναῶν ἱππίου τʼ Ἄργους κτίται ,
sections.translation: O Danaid earth ! Dwellers in Argos , city of horses , put on your armor and come to help ! For this fellow is forcing his life from your whole city , though he has caused pollution by shedding his mother ’ s blood .

sections.label: Section 1625
sections.translation: Appearing in the clouds . Menelaus , calm your anger that has been whetted ; I am Phoebus , the son of Leto , drawing near to call you by name . And you also , Orestes , who are keeping guard on the girl , sword in hand , so that you may hear what I have come to say . Helen , whom all your eagerness

sections.label: Section 1630
sections.translation: failed to destroy , when you were seeking to anger Menelaus , is here as you see in the enfolding air , rescued from death and not slain by you . I saved her and snatched her from beneath your sword at the bidding of father Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 1635
sections.original: Ζηνὸς γὰρ οὖσαν ζῆν νιν ἄφθιτον χρεών ,
sections.translation: for she , his child , must be immortal , and take her seat with Castor and Polydeuces in the enfolding air , a savior to mariners . Choose another bride and take her to your home ; for the gods by that one ’ s loveliness

sections.label: Section 1640
sections.original: Ἕλληνας εἰς ἓν καὶ Φρύγας συνήγαγον ,
sections.translation: joined Troy and Hellas in battle , causing death so that they might draw off from the earth the outrage of unstinting numbers of mortals .

sections.label: Section 1643
sections.original: τὰ μὲν καθʼ Ἑλένην ὧδʼ ἔχει · σὲ δʼ αὖ χρεών ,
sections.translation: So much for Helen ; as for you , Orestes , you must cross the broders of this land

sections.label: Section 1645
sections.translation: and dwell for one whole year on Parrhasian soil , which from your flight shall be called the land of Orestes by Azanians and Arcadians . And when you return from there to the city of Athens , undergo your trial by the Avenging Three for your mother ’ s murder ;

sections.label: Section 1650
sections.original: Εὐμενίσι τρισσαῖς · θεοὶ δέ σοι δίκης βραβῆς
sections.translation: the gods will be arbitrators of your trial , and will take a most righteous vote on you at the hill of Ares , where you are to win your case . And it is destined , Orestes , that you will marry Hermione , at whose neck you are holding your sword ;

sections.label: Section 1655
sections.original: Νεοπτόλεμος γαμεῖν νιν , οὐ γαμεῖ ποτε .
sections.translation: Neoptolemus shall never marry her , though he thinks he will ; for he is fated to die by a Delphian sword , when he claims satisfaction of me for the death of his father Achilles . Give your sister in marriage to Pylades , to whom you formerly promised her ; the life awaiting him is one of happiness .

sections.label: Section 1660
sections.original: Ἄργους δʼ Ὀρέστην , Μενέλεως , ἔα κρατεῖν ,
sections.translation: Menelaus , leave Orestes to rule Argos ; go and reign over the Spartan land , keeping it as the dowry of a wife who till this day never ceased causing you innumerable troubles . I will set matters straight between Orestes and the citizens ,

sections.label: Section 1665
sections.translation: for I forced him to murder his mother .

sections.label: Section 1666
sections.translation: Hail , prophetic Loxias , for your oracles ! You were not a lying prophet after all , but a true seer ; and yet I was afraid that it was some fiend I had listened to , when I seemed to hear your voice ;

sections.label: Section 1670
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εὖ τελεῖται , πείσομαι δὲ σοῖς λόγοις .
sections.translation: but all is ending well , and I obey your word . There ! I release Hermione from slaughter and agree to make her my wife whenever her father gives her .

sections.label: Section 1673
sections.original: ὦ Ζηνὸς Ἑλένη χαῖρε παῖ · ζηλῶ δέ σε
sections.translation: All hail , Helen , daughter of Zeus ! I wish you joy of your home in heaven ’ s happy courts .

sections.label: Section 1675
sections.original: Ὀρέστα , σοὶ δὲ παῖδʼ ἐγὼ κατεγγυῶ ,
sections.translation: To you , Orestes , I betroth my daughter , as Phoebus said ; being noble yourself , may you have benefit from a noble wife , and may I also , in giving her to you .

sections.label: Section 1678
sections.translation: Go now each one to the place appointed by me ; reconcile your quarrels .

sections.label: Section 1679b
sections.translation: I must obey .

sections.label: Section 1680
sections.translation: And so must I ; I make a truce with my fate , Menelaus , and with your oracles , Loxias .

sections.label: Section 1682
sections.original: ἴτε νυν καθʼ ὁδόν , τὴν καλλίστην
sections.translation: Go your ways , and honor Peace , fairest of goddesses ; I will bring Helen to the halls of Zeus ,

sections.label: Section 1685
sections.translation: when I have come to the sky , bright with stars . There , enthroned beside Hera and Hebe , the bride of Heracles , she will be honored by men with libations as a goddess for ever ; along with those Zeus-born sons of Tyndareus ,

sections.label: Section 1690
sections.translation: she will be a guardian of the sea , for the good of sailors .

sections.label: Section 1691
sections.original: ὦ μέγα σεμνὴ Νίκη , τὸν ἐμὸν
sections.translation: Greatly revered Victory , may you occupy my life , and never cease to crown me !
